title: linaria amethystea var. broussonetii
intro: Linaria amethystea var. broussonetii, also known as the Purple Toadflax, is a perennial plant that belongs to the figwort family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a demulcent and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes. The Purple Toadflax is said to possess health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations like infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
